zh.lifeSummary: 當Mathilde Maud Prisett於1450年出生在英國盧特藍德艾可頓時，她的父親Sir John Priset32歲，母親Lady Maud Plumpton28歲。她於1470年與Robert De Harrington在英國結婚。他們至少是6個兒子的父母。她去世於1500年在她的家鄉享年50歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭盧特藍德艾可頓。

nameMeanings.name: Prisett
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from a place in Hampshire, which probably gets its name from an unrecorded Old English word pryfet ‘privet’. This word is found from an early date in placenames, for example Privett Farm in Standlynch, Wiltshire, which could be a source of the surname, but as a vocabulary element it is not recorded before the 16th century.

nameMeanings.name: Mathilde
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Matilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

nameMeanings.name: Maud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Mathilde Maud Prisett was born in 1450, in Exton, Rutland, England, her father, Sir John Priset, was 32 and her mother, Lady Maud Plumpton, was 28. She married Robert De Harrington in 1470, in England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons. She died in 1500, in her hometown, at the age of 50, and was buried in Exton, Rutland, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Mathilde Maud Prisett est née en 1450, à Exton, Rutland, Angleterre, son père, Sir John Priset, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Lady Maud Plumpton, avait 28 ans. Elle a épousé Robert De Harrington en 1470, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils. Elle est décédée en 1500, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 50 ans, et a été enterrée à Exton, Rutland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ko.lifeSummary: Mathilde Maud Prisett(이)가 1450년 영국 루틀랜드 스턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John Priset(은)는 32세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Maud Plumpton(은)는 28세였다. 그녀는 1470년 영국에서 Robert De Harrington(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1500년 그녀의 고향에서 50세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 루틀랜드 스턴에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Mathilde Maud Prisett родилась в 1450 г. в местности экстон, рутланд, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir John Priset было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Lady Maud Plumpton, 28. Она умерла в 1500 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 50 лет и была похоронена в местности экстон, рутланд, Англия, Великобритания.
